source.title: Staged docs for Spending by Award

observations.statement: The documented endpoint is POST /api/v2/search/spending_by_award/ on https://api.usaspending.gov and is described as the advanced-search award listing surface for complex spending filters.

Documented top-level request body members are filters and fields as required inputs plus optional limit, order, page, sort, subawards, last_record_unique_id, last_record_sort_value, and spending_level.
The docs say limit defaults to 10, order defaults to desc, sort defaults to the first requested field, subawards defaults to false, and spending_level defaults to awards.
The fields array is a request-time selection of API-defined output labels and changes with award-versus-subaward mode plus award-type family.

source.title: AdvancedFilterObject section for Spending by Award

observations.statement: The documented AdvancedFilterObject members are keywords, description, time_period, place_of_performance_scope, place_of_performance_locations, agencies, recipient_search_text, recipient_scope, recipient_locations, recipient_type_names, award_type_codes, award_ids, award_amounts, program_numbers, naics_codes, tas_codes, psc_codes, contract_pricing_type_codes, set_aside_type_codes, extent_competed_type_codes, treasury_account_components, program_activity, program_activities, def_codes, and award_unique_id.
The docs mark award_type_codes as required within filters and describe quoted award_ids as exact-match searches instead of fuzzier full-text matching.
TimePeriodObject is polymorphic: award-mode requests use award search time periods while subaward-mode requests use subaward search time periods from the shared search_filters documentation.
AgencyObject requires type, tier, and name, with optional toptier_name for subtier disambiguation; ProgramActivityObject requires at least one of name or code when used.

NAICSCodeObject supports require/exclude arrays, PSCCodeObject and TASCodeObject support structured require/exclude arrays-of-arrays, and TreasuryAccountComponentsObject requires aid and main while ata, bpoa, epoa, a, and sub are optional.

The docs describe special DEFC behavior: prime-award searches with COVID-19 or IIJA def_codes only return awards with matching File C data and non-zero related obligations or outlays, while subaward searches apply enactment-date restrictions to sub_action_date.

source.title: Documented response and field catalog for Spending by Award

observations.statement: The documented 200 response contains spending_level, limit, results, page_metadata, and optional messages.
The documented PageMetadataObject includes page, hasNext, last_record_unique_id, and last_record_sort_value.

The documented SpendingByAwardResponse always includes internal_id and may include requested award, assistance, or subaward field-label columns such as Award ID, Recipient Name, Award Amount, Awarding Agency, Total Outlays, Contract Award Type, Award Type, Prime Award ID, Prime Recipient Name, Sub-Award ID, Sub-Award Amount, and Sub-Awardee Name.

The documented field catalog also includes helper identifiers such as generated_internal_id, recipient_id, prime_award_recipient_id, prime_award_internal_id, and prime_award_generated_internal_id plus optional agency_slug when Awarding Agency is returned.

The field catalog documents object-like labels such as NAICS, PSC, Recipient Location, Primary Place of Performance, Sub-Recipient Location, Sub-Award Primary Place of Performance, Assistance Listings, and primary_assistance_listing, but the response section does not fully describe their nested members.

source.title: Current raw profile for Spending by Award

observations.statement: The current raw MCP profile exposes only top-level request properties filters, fields, page, limit, sort, and order, with filters described generically rather than as a nested documented surface.

The current raw profile omits documented top-level controls subawards, spending_level, last_record_unique_id, and last_record_sort_value and does not enumerate important nested filter members such as filters.time_period, filters.award_type_codes, filters.award_ids, filters.agencies, filters.recipient_search_text, filters.recipient_locations, filters.def_codes, and filters.award_unique_id.

The current raw profile's semantic prompt already warns that generated_internal_id is the safer award-detail follow-up key than the display Award ID, but that join guidance is not encoded in the raw input schema itself.

source.title: USAspending source for Spending by Award

observations.statement: Source code sets spending_level to awards by default, flips to subawards when the legacy subawards boolean is true, and validates fields plus award_type_codes as required request members.
Pagination source rules define page default 1, limit default 10 with max 100, order default desc, and the view defaults sort to the first requested field when sort is omitted.

The view rejects sort keys that are not valid for the selected spending_level and award_type_codes, except for a small allowlist of object-valued labels such as NAICS, PSC, Recipient Location, Primary Place of Performance, Assistance Listing(s), Sub-Recipient Location, and Sub-Award Primary Place of Performance.

Prime-award responses always add internal_id and generated_internal_id, overwrite Award ID with display_award_id when requested, and add awarding_agency_id plus agency_slug when Awarding Agency is present.

Subaward responses add internal_id, prime_award_internal_id, and prime_award_generated_internal_id, and complex labels like NAICS, PSC, Sub-Recipient Location, and Sub-Award Primary Place of Performance are emitted as objects rather than simple scalar strings.

The Elasticsearch pagination implementation requires last_record_unique_id and last_record_sort_value together for search_after paging and populates both values in page_metadata when another sequential page is available.

source.title: Derived MCP coverage and workflow assessment

observations.statement: This endpoint is the detailed advanced-search row surface: each result row represents one award or one subaward depending on spending_level and the selected field catalog, not an aggregate total.

Because the current raw profile does not enumerate nested filters or cursor pagination controls, a caller relying only on the raw MCP surface would miss materially supported request paths such as spending_level, subawards, last_record_unique_id, last_record_sort_value, filters.time_period, filters.award_type_codes, filters.agencies, filters.recipient_search_text, filters.recipient_locations, filters.def_codes, and filters.award_unique_id.
The most reusable downstream join from award-mode results is generated_internal_id, which the source and current prompt tie to generated unique award IDs rather than the human display Award ID.
Some request-time field labels map to structured objects in the response, so callers should not assume every requested cell is a plain string or number even when the docs list only a label name.

source.title: Live baseline award-mode probe for Spending by Award

results.Recipient Name: STATE STREET BANK AND TRUST COMPANY
results.Awarding Agency: Department of Housing and Urban Development

messages: For searches, time period start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.  For data going back to 2000-10-01, use either the Custom Award Download feature on the website or one of our download or bulk_download API endpoints as listed on https://api.usaspending.gov/docs/endpoints.

observations.statement: A baseline award-mode request with contract award_type_codes, a time_period filter, selected fields, page=1, limit=1, sort=Award ID, and order=asc returned HTTP 200.
When spending_level was omitted, the live response reported spending_level=awards, confirming the documented default award mode.
The live award row included internal_id, Award ID, Recipient Name, Award Amount, Awarding Agency, awarding_agency_id, agency_slug, and generated_internal_id.
page_metadata included page=1, hasNext=true, last_record_unique_id, and last_record_sort_value, demonstrating that the endpoint emits the documented cursor pair when another page is available.
The live response included the shared search warning that search start and end dates are currently limited to an earliest date of 2007-10-01.

source.title: Reviewer report for spending_by_award high-value screening template
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-1-search-high-value-template for v2__search__spending_by_award

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that getRequestTemplate(v2__search__spending_by_award, useCase=high_value_award_screening) returned the generic award-search-baseline template, which sorted by Award ID ascending instead of Award Amount descending.

The reviewer also reported that validateRequest accepted a manually repaired high-value screening request with Award Amount descending and no warnings, indicating the problem was template quality rather than endpoint capability.

source.title: Reviewer report for spending_by_award high-value screening template
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-1-search-high-value-template for v2__search__spending_by_award

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that a bounded contract-only request with award_type_codes [A, B, C, D], time_period 2025-10-01 through 2026-09-30, fields [Award ID, Recipient Name, Award Amount, Awarding Agency], page=1, limit=5, sort=Award Amount, and order=desc validated cleanly and returned HTTP 200.

That same reviewer-backed live screen surfaced HT940216C0001 for HUMANA GOVERNMENT BUSINESS INC with Award Amount 51269205263.03 at the top and still included generated_internal_id CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE- for downstream award-detail follow-up even though generated_internal_id was not part of the requested fields array.

source.title: Reviewer report for spending_by_award canonical award lookup alias
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2-canonical-award-id-alias for v2__search__spending_by_award

observations.statement: The reviewer-backed drilldown selected a prime-award row with display Award ID HT940216C0001 and generated_internal_id CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE- in the same search result.
The reviewer reported that award-detail guidance explicitly says generated_internal_id is safer than the display Award ID when chaining from spending_by_award.

source.title: Reviewer report for spending_by_award canonical award lookup alias
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-2-canonical-award-id-alias for v2__search__spending_by_award

observations.statement: The reviewer reported that the same generated value from search carried successfully into v2__awards__award_id and v2__awards__funding even though those related endpoints label the handoff field differently.

In the reviewer-backed workflow, v2__awards__funding accepted the search-generated hash under request field name award_id, while award detail discusses generated_unique_award_id or generated_internal_id for the same business key.

source.title: Reviewer report for spending_by_award explicit canonical handoff response fact
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting RT-1 for v2__search__spending_by_award

observations.statement: The selected RT-1 reviewer report said the high_value_award_screening template requests only the visible fields Award ID, Recipient Name, Award Amount, and Awarding Agency, so the downstream machine handoff key must be documented separately from that visible projection.

The same reviewer-backed live search row still included generated_internal_id CONT_AWD_HT940216C0001_9700_-NONE-_-NONE- even though it was not in the requested fields array, and that value carried successfully into award detail and funding follow-ups.

RT-1 requires the promoted bundle itself to expose canonical_award_lookup_id as an explicit response-side semantic fact so agents can treat the helper as an intended downstream handoff field rather than an incidental extra column.

source.title: Reviewer report for spending_by_award time_period interpretation caveat
source.locator: Structured reviewer report selecting repair-search-period-meaning-caveat for v2__search__spending_by_award

observations.statement: The reviewer-backed FY2026 high_value_award_screening request for 2025-10-01 through 2026-09-30 returned contract award DENA0003525 to NATIONAL TECHNOLOGY & ENGINEERING SOLUTIONS OF SANDIA, LLC with Award Amount 42111665692.01, showing that the bounded current-period screen can surface this row.

The same reviewer-backed downstream award-detail drilldown for the carried canonical id reported date_signed 2016-12-16 and period_of_performance 2017-01-18 through 2027-04-30, so the period-bounded search surfaced a long-running award active in the period rather than a newly signed FY2026 contract.

Together, the paired search and award-detail evidence means a bounded time_period filter in this workflow should be interpreted as screening for awards with relevant period activity, while Award Amount remains an award-level value rather than a period-only measure.
